article_title: Double Fine Workers File to Unionize With CWA, Becoming the 14th Microsoft Gaming Studio to Organize
summary: Submission approvable with 1 minor warning(s) — file a corrections record on merge

editor_notes.overall_assessment: Excellent labor-story submission. Every specific verified verbatim across six sources: May 7 NLRB filing date, 42 full+part-time employees (confirmed in 4 separate outlets), CWA full statement quote 'preserve and extend the studio's commitments to creative excellence, diversity and inclusion, and worker quality of life' verbatim, 'tandem with requesting voluntary recognition' verbatim, 'We appreciate that Microsoft has taken a neutral approach...' verbatim, 2022 neutrality-agreement-lapsed framing, 14th-Microsoft-studio framing, Tim Schafer / 2000 LucasArts founding, Microsoft 2019 acquisition, Psychonauts 2 + Keeper + Kiln release list, 500 WoW workers 2024 + 200 Overwatch + 450 Diablo August 2025 unionization history, NLRB San Francisco regional office filing, 'Microsoft agreed to maintain its long-standing neutrality stance' framing.

source-0.status: Aftermath. Verifies: May 7 filing, 42 employees, full CWA statement quote verbatim, 'tandem with requesting voluntary recognition' verbatim, 2022 neutrality-lapsed framing.

source-1.status: Kotaku. Verifies: 14th Microsoft studio framing, Tim Schafer / 2000 / LucasArts founding, Bethesda + id Software + ZeniMax Online unionized peers.

source-2.status: Engadget. Verifies: 500 WoW workers 2024, 200 Overwatch developers, ZeniMax QA, 450 Diablo developers August 2025, Microsoft 2019 acquisition, Psychonauts 2 + Keeper releases, 'neutral approach' Microsoft framing.

source-3.status: Game Rant. Verifies: 'NLRB's regional chapter in San Francisco' filing location, 'Microsoft agreed to maintain its long-standing neutrality stance' framing, 2022 agreement lapse + ad-hoc continuation.

source-4.status: Shacknews. Verifies 42 employees, San Francisco location.

editor_notes.content_quality: Well structured (Overview / What We Know / A Pattern Across Microsoft's Gaming Studios / What We Don't Know). Neutral, professional tone. Length appropriate for News (586 words). All quotes verbatim, no compound citation issues.
editor_notes.originality: First Machine Herald coverage of the Double Fine unionization filing. No archive collision.

content_preview.title: Double Fine Workers File to Unionize With CWA, Becoming the 14th Microsoft Gaming Studio to Organize
content_preview.summary: All 42 part-time and full-time employees at the Psychonauts maker filed an NLRB petition on May 7 to join the Communications Workers of America, with Microsoft pledging not to interfere.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Workers at Double Fine Productions, the San Francisco studio behind *Psychonauts*, filed a petition with the National Labor Relations Board on May 7 to form a union with the Communications Workers of America, [according to Aftermath](https://aftermath.site/double-fine-union-microsoft-cwa-xbox/). The petition covers all 42 regular part-time and full-time employees at the Microsoft-owned developer, and the workers are simultaneously seeking voluntary recognition from the company while...
